title: Andrea Bocelli Tells Timothée Chalamet Opera, Ballet and Filmmaking Draw From the ‘Same Source’ of Human Emotion: ‘They Are Not Arts of the Past’
content:
ByJack Dunn

Italian opera legend Andrea Bocelli toldPeoplethat he’s “surprised” his “fellow artist” Timothée Chalamet disparaged the value of opera and ballet during the “Variety& CNN Town Hall,” adding that both art forms are very much alive and draw from the “same source” of human emotion as filmmaking.

“I believe we often tend to keep our distance from what we have not yet truly encountered,” Bocelli said. “Opera and ballet are art forms that have crossed centuries and continue to speak to the human heart, because they answer a deep need for beauty, truth, and emotion. They are not arts of the past, but living languages that can still move us, make us reflect, and bring different generations together.”

Bocelli is considered one of the great modern authorities on opera singing and has sold more than 90 million records during his career. The five-time Grammy nominee went on to invite Chalamet to one of his opera performances if he’s ever curious to learn more about the medium.

“I am convinced that a sensitive performer like Timothée, who understands the power of emotions, may one day discover that opera and dance draw from that very same source,” he added. “Should he ever be curious, I would be happy to welcome him as a guest at one of my concerts. Sometimes it only takes a few minutes of hearing this music live to understand why, after centuries, it continues to be loved all over the world.”

Bocelli is hardly the first in the opera community to fire back at Chalamet for his viral comment.Several opera housesfrom across the globe took to social media and issued a response, with most of them inviting the “Marty Supreme” star to a show. On Feb. 5,London’s Royal Ballet and Operatook to Instagram to share footage of its craftspeople and performers in an apparent response to Chalamet. The caption of the post read, “Every night at the Royal Opera House, thousands of people gather for ballet and opera. For the music. For the storytelling. For the sheer magic of live performance. If you’d like to reconsider, [Timothée Chalamet], our doors are open.”

In the full quote from Chalamet’s “Town Hall” conversation with Matthew McConaughey, the “Complete Unknown” star weighed the role of movie theaters in the modern entertainment landscape. He said that the popularity of a “serious movie” like Guillermo del Toro’s “Frankenstein” shows that audiences still crave nuanced storytelling suited best for the big screen, but at the same time, he understands that “some people want to be entertained and quickly.”

“I’m really right in the middle, Matthew,”Chalamet said. “Cause I admire people, and I’ve done it myself, [who] go on a talk show and go, ‘Hey, we gotta keep movie theaters alive. You know, we gotta keep this genre alive.’ And another part of me feels like, if people wanna see it, like ‘Barbie,’ like ‘Oppenheimer,’ they’re gonna go see it and go out of their way and be loud and proud about it. And I don’t want to be working in ballet or opera, or things where it’s like, ‘Hey, keep this thing alive.’ Even though it’s like, no one cares about this anymore. All respect to the ballet and opera people out there. I just lost 14 cents in viewership.”
title_zh: 安德烈·波切利(Andrea Bocelli)告訴蒂莫西·查拉梅(Timothée Chalamet)，歌劇、芭蕾舞和電影創作都源自於人類情感的「同一源頭」： 「它們不是過去的藝術。」
content_zh:
意大利歌劇傳奇安德烈·波切利(Andrea Bocelli)在接受《人物》(People)雜誌訪問時表示，他對他的“藝術夥伴”提摩西·查拉梅(Timothée Chalamet)在“綜藝與CNN市政廳”(Variety & CNN Town Hall)中貶低歌劇和芭蕾舞的價值感到“驚訝”，並補充說這兩種藝術形式依然充滿活力，並與電影製作源自相同的人類情感。

波切利說：“我相信我們往往會對尚未真正接觸的事物保持距離。歌劇和芭蕾舞是跨越世紀的藝術形式，繼續觸動人心，因為它們滿足了對美、真理和情感的深切需求。它們不是過去的藝術，而是仍然能夠感動我們、讓我們反思並將不同世代聚集在一起的活生生的語言。”

波切利被認為是現代歌劇演唱的偉大權威之一，在他的職業生涯中售出了超過9000萬張唱片。這位五次格萊美獎提名者隨後邀請查拉梅參加他的歌劇表演，如果他對這種媒介有興趣的話。

他補充道：“我相信像提摩西這樣敏感的表演者，理解情感的力量，可能有一天會發現歌劇和舞蹈源自同一來源。如果他有興趣，我會很高興在我的音樂會上歡迎他作為嘉賓。有時，只需幾分鐘的現場音樂，就能理解為什麼這些音樂在幾個世紀後仍然受到全世界的喜愛。”

波切利並不是歌劇界第一位對查拉梅的病毒性評論作出反擊的人。來自全球的幾個歌劇院在社交媒體上發表了回應，大多數邀請這位“馬蒂·至尊”(Marty Supreme)明星參加演出。2月5日，倫敦皇家芭蕾舞團(Royal Ballet)和歌劇院(Opera)在Instagram上分享了其工匠和表演者的影片，顯然是對查拉梅的回應。該帖子的說明寫道：“在皇家歌劇院，每晚都有數千人聚集欣賞芭蕾舞和歌劇。為了音樂。為了講故事。為了現場表演的純粹魔力。如果你想重新考慮，[提摩西·查拉梅]，我們的大門隨時為你敞開。”

在查拉梅與馬修·麥康納(Matthew McConaughey)的“市政廳”對話的完整引用中，這位“完全陌生”(Complete Unknown)的明星權衡了電影劇院在現代娛樂格局中的角色。他表示，像吉爾莫·德爾·托羅(Guillermo del Toro)的“科學怪人”(Frankenstein)這樣的“嚴肅電影”的受歡迎程度顯示觀眾仍然渴望適合大銀幕的細膩敘事，但同時，他也理解“有些人想要快速娛樂”。

“我真的處於中間地帶，馬修(Matthew)，”查拉梅(Chalamet)說。“因為我欣賞那些人，我自己也這麼做過，[他們]上脫口秀說，‘嘿，我們必須讓電影院活下去。你知道，我們必須讓這個類型活下去。’而我內心的另一部分則覺得，如果人們想看，就像《芭比(Barbie)》和《奧本海默(Oppenheimer)》，他們會去看，並且會努力去看，並為此感到驕傲。我不想在芭蕾舞或歌劇等地方工作，或是那些需要說‘嘿，讓這個東西活下去’的地方。儘管這樣說，實際上沒有人再關心這些了。對於那些芭蕾舞和歌劇的人我表示尊重。我剛剛在觀眾數上損失了14美分。”
summary_zh: 意大利歌劇傳奇安德烈·波切利(Andrea Bocelli)在接受《人物》(People)雜誌訪問時表示，他對他的“藝術夥伴”提摩西·查拉梅(Timothée Chalamet)在“綜藝與CNN市政廳”(Variety & CNN Town Hall)中貶低歌劇和芭蕾舞的價值感到“驚訝”，並補充說這兩種藝術形式依然充滿活力，並與電影製作源自相同的人類情感。
